notes: mistral-vibe variant: one turn runs, the session exits cleanly, then is relaunched via `vibe --resume <SESSION_ID>` in the SAME cwd. Resume rehydrates through resume_existing_session, which reopens the ORIGINAL session_<ts>_<shortid> directory and APPENDS to its messages.jsonl (session_logger.py:407, cli.py:208-218) — no new dir is minted. The adapter keys the daemon session off that directory basename (sessionIDFromPath), so the SAME session_id row reappears across both PID lifetimes — ONE session id, TWO PIDs. vibe writes messages.jsonl lazily (save_interaction, after the first turn), so the initial ready is satisfied by the process-detected proc-<PID> presession; v1_session_birth anchors UNPINNED to start so that transient row cannot steal the birth, and the turn/exit/resume phases pin to v1_turn_start via same_session_as. Observations: model harvested from the meta.json sidecar (config.active_model=mistral-medium-3.5, confirmed across all six live transcripts + config default; NormalizeModelName leaves it unchanged); tokens non-zero from stats.context_tokens on turn_done; cost NOT asserted (the daemon computes no per-turn cost for vibe). PROVISIONAL — parser+source read, no recording; record settles the lazy-write emission timing and the clean-exit process_exited (PID-binding dependent), and re-anchors if a proc-<PID> presession model surfaces.

text: First launch: session appears in ready (proc-<PID> presession satisfies ready before the lazy transcript materializes)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Clean exit after the first turn: process_exited for the session (PID bound by DiscoverPID via CommandPattern + cwd)
Spec: 'Same session_id reappears (not a new session row)' — `vibe --resume <id>` reopens the SAME session_<ts>_<shortid> directory, so the SAME session id re-binds to a new PID in ready
Spec: 'ready → working on the first new turn' — the second turn drives the SAME session to working
Second turn completes back to ready on the SAME session — each lifetime's arc is independently correct
